### Post by killaray on 2006-11-03
the TV out works during start up and shut down, but as soon as OS starts up it just start flickering... anyone know wut it could be and how to fix?

---

### Post by Titus A Duxass on 2006-11-03
Possibly your refresh rates, post your xorg.conf file and we'll take a look.

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-03
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AS [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"TVOutput" "NTSC"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AS [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480" "1x1"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-06
can anyone help with this? do u think it can be the framerate that cause the flickering? to me it doesnt seem like anything that has to do with framerate but i rather have a linux pro opinion

---

### Post by jsandys on 2006-11-06
First install the fglrx drivers.  Then run the command aticonfig --initial, then aticonfig --help, print out the help, this will give you a clue to what configuration options to use.  When you use the TV options the aticonfig will set the proper refresh rates.

It looks like from your xorg.conf that your TV is your only monitor.  This can work, but the max resolution you are going to get is 800x600 if you are lucky.

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-06
im using an actual monitor, y would it say my tv is the only monitor?

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-07
this is the error i get tryin to install the fglrx drivers ```
./ati-installer.sh: 156: Syntax error: Bad substitution
Removing temporary directory: fglrx-install
```

---

### Post by jsandys on 2006-11-07
Use easyubuntu to install the ATI driver or select it by searching for "fglrx" in Synaptic.  with ./ati... it sounds like you are trying to install the proprietary ATI driver.  I would avoid that.  While the fglrx (ATI) driver from the Ubuntu repository is not the very latest, it is configured to work with your version version of the kernel and installs with Synaptic.

You have the TV output option in xorg.conf, I couldn't get both good TV out and good 3d performance from an ATI card.

---

but my only issue now is that videos wont show up on my tv when i play them... the regular stuff is showing but videos stay black

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-07
you need to change the output to gl, openGL, or X11openGL in your media player(s).

Look for output in options/prefs.

I suggest installing mplayer, it is the easiest to configure, and once that is setup, all the other players seem to fall into place automatically.

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-07
got that to work on MPlayer but when i full screen it slows the video down.. any thoughts?

---

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-07
```
when i full screen it slows the video down.
```

..it shouldn't.

try adding:

```
"UseInternalAGPGART"  "no"
```

to the end of /etc/X11/xorg.conf

..then add:

```
agpgart
fglrx
dri
```

to the end of /etc/modules

..reboot.

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-08
cant edit modules wont let me save...

_edit_
forget it got it workin under nautilus

---

### Post by killaray on 2006-11-08
ok everything is back to speed with ```
"UseInternalAGPGART"  "no"
``` edit.. u guys r great help.. i appreciate it... but i have 1 more question before im dont with this thread... in windows when u had dual screen, 1 was always blank and u had to drag stuff over such as the video player to view movies and such on there.. but on ubuntu it seems that both screens r the same so wut im doin now can be seen on the monitor and TV... is it supposed to be that way?

---

### Post by kvonb on 2006-11-08
Glad to hear that it worked.

As for the dual screen, you will have to look up "dual monitor", I don't think the driver supports using the TVout as a second screen though.
